ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, PETITION OF BRYON HOSKINDS TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF LARRY HOSKINDS BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION IS GRANTED UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2035.010. ALL PARTIES MUST FILE A NOTICE OF SPECIAL APPEARANCE WITHIN TWO COURT DAYS UPON RECEIPT OF THIS ORDER. THE DEPOSITION IS TO TAKE PLACE BY ZOOM TECHNOLOGY WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE ENTRY OF THIS ORDER UNLESS THE PARTIES AGREE OTHERWISE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, CLERK: A. MOK. NOT REPORTED. (503)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Bryon Hoskinds To Perpetuate Testimony Of Larry Hoskinds By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Gilbert L. Purcell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing in person for petitioner Bryon Hoskinds.
Stephen L. Jenkins of GOLDBERG SEGALLA LLP (415) 432-6600) appearing for defendant Tremco Inc.
James H. Mokhtarzadeh of GORDON & REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (510-463-8600), appearing for defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corporation.
Nicole B. Yuen of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant C.R. Laurence Co., Inc.
Michael E. Sandgren of HUSCH BLACKWELL LLC (510-768-0650), appearing for defendant MorseTec, LLC.

RULING:
Not contested, not argued and tentative ruling adopted: The Petition of Bryon Hoskinds to Perpetuate Testimony of Larry Hoskinds by Videotaped Deposition is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 2035.010. Petitioner filed proofs of service as to all prospective defendants named in the petition. Specially appearing party Air & Liquid Systems Corporation's request for judicial notice is granted as to Larry Hoskinds' Complaint from San Francisco Superior Court Case Number CGC-18-276721 filed August 30, 2018. (Exhibit A; Evidence Code section 452(d).) The request for judicial notice is denied as to the Los Angeles Superior Court order in In re: Petition of John T. Verant, Case Number BS136198 filed August 27, 2012. (Exhibit B.) Orders from other trial courts are irrelevant to this Court's decision and not binding on this Court. All parties must file a Notice of Special Appearance within two court days upon receipt of this order. The deposition is to take place by Zoom technology within 30 days of the entry of this order unless the parties agree otherwise. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
